samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>An asynchronous FIFO implemented with a dual-port RAM module, including controllers for writing and reading data, as well as synchronizers for the read and write pointers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Used to trigger write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Used to trigger read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data to be written into the FIFO.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data read from the FIFO.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH" value_description="16">FIFO width configuration.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH" value_description="16">FIFO depth configuration.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <!-- Dual Port RAM Instantiation -->
    <task>
        <step id="1">Instantiate a dual port RAM named "dual_port_RAM".</step>
        <step id="2">Connect the RAM's wclk input to the write clock signal.</step>
        <step id="3">Connect the RAM's wenc input to the write enable signal (winc).</step>
        <step id="4">Connect the RAM's waddr input to the write address (waddr).</step>
        <step id="5">Connect the RAM's wdata input to the write data input (wdata).</step>
        <step id="6">Connect the RAM's rclk input to the read clock signal.</step>
        <step id="7">Connect the RAM's renc input to the read enable signal (rinc).</step>
        <step id="8">Connect the RAM's raddr input to the read address (raddr).</step>
        <step id="9">Connect the RAM's rdata output to the read data output (rdata).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Data Write Controller Instantiation -->
    <task>
        <step id="10">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "write_controller".</step>
        <step id="11">Declare a register variable waddr_bin representing the binary value of the write address (waddr) in the range [DEPTH-1:0].</step>
        <step id="12">Initialize the write address (waddr) with the least significant bit (LSB) of the waddr_bin register.</step>
        <step id="13">Assign the next value of the write address (waddr) by shifting the LSB up to three positions to the left (i.e., adding powers of two).</step>
        <step id="14">Assign the wen signal to be active when the write enable signal (winc) is asserted and the RAM is not full.</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Data Read Controller Instantiation -->
    <task>
        <step id="15">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "read_controller".</step>
        <step id="16">Declare a register variable raddr_bin representing the binary value of the read address (raddr) in the range [DEPTH-1:0].</step>
        <step id="17">Initialize the read address (raddr) with the least significant bit (LSB) of the raddr_bin register.</step>
        <step id="18">Assign the next value of the read address (raddr) by shifting the LSB up to three positions to the left (i.e., adding powers of two).</step>
        <step id="19">Assign the ren signal to be active when the read enable signal (rinc) is asserted and the RAM is not empty.</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Synchronizer Blocks for Read Pointer Instantiation -->
    <task>
        <step id="20">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "read_pointer_synchronizer".</step>
        <step id="21">Declare a register variable rptr_syn representing the synchronized version of the read pointer (rptr) in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="22">Initialize the read pointer synchronization register (rptr_syn) with the read pointer (rptr).</step>
        <step id="23">On the rising edge of the read clock (posedge rclk), update the read pointer synchronization register (rptr_syn) with the current value of the read pointer (rptr).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Synchronizer Blocks for Write Pointer Instantiation -->
    <task>
        <step id="24">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "write_pointer_synchronizer".</step>
        <step id="25">Declare a register variable wptr_syn representing the synchronized version of the write pointer (wptr) in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="26">Initialize the write pointer synchronization register (wptr_syn) with the write pointer (wptr).</step>
        <step id="27">On the rising edge of the write clock (posedge wclk), update the write pointer synchronization register (wptr_syn) with the current value of the write pointer (wptr).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Full Signal Generation Logic -->
    <task>
        <step id="28">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "generate_full_signal".</step>
        <step id="29">Declare a register variable wfull_gen representing the generated full signal in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="30">Initialize the full signal generation register (wfull_gen) with 0.</step>
        <step id="31">Compare the write pointer (wptr) with the synchronized read pointer (rptr_syn).</step>
        <step id="32">Update the full signal generation register (wfull_gen) accordingly.</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Empty Signal Generation Logic -->
    <task>
        <step id="33">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "generate_empty_signal".</step>
        <step id="34">Declare a register variable rempty_gen representing the generated empty signal in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="35">Initialize the empty signal generation register (rempty_gen) with 0.</step>
        <step id="36">Compare the read pointer (rptr) with the synchronized write pointer (wptr_syn).</step>
        <step id="37">Update the empty signal generation register (rempty_gen) accordingly.</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Output Assignments -->
    <task>
        <step id="38">Assign the full signal (wfull) to the generated full signal (wfull_gen).</step>
        <step id="39">Assign the empty signal (rempty) to the generated empty signal (rempty_gen).</step>
        <step id="40">Assign the read data output (rdata) to the read data from the dual port RAM (rdata).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Binary Encoding and Gray Code Conversion for Write Address (waddr) -->
    <task>
        <step id="41">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "binary_encode_waddr_to_gray_code".</step>
        <step id="42">Declare a register variable waddr_bin_encoded representing the encoded binary value of the write address (waddr) in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="43">Initialize the binary encoding of the write address (waddr) with its least significant bit (LSB).</step>
        <step id="44">Perform the binary encoding process by iterating through the bits of the write address (waddr), converting them to Gray code using XOR operations with right-shifted values, and storing the result in the binary encoding register (waddr_bin_encoded).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Binary Encoding and Gray Code Conversion for Read Address (raddr) -->
    <task>
        <step id="45">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "binary_encode_raddr_to_gray_code".</step>
        <step id="46">Declare a register variable raddr_bin_encoded representing the encoded binary value of the read address (raddr) in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="47">Initialize the binary encoding of the read address (raddr) with its least significant bit (LSB).</step>
        <step id="48">Perform the binary encoding process by iterating through the bits of the read address (raddr), converting them to Gray code using XOR operations with right-shifted values, and storing the result in the binary encoding register (raddr_bin_encoded).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Buffer Register for Write Pointer (wptr) -->
    <task>
        <step id="49">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "buffer_write_pointer".</step>
        <step id="50">Declare a register variable wptr_buff representing the buffered write pointer in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="51">Initialize the buffered write pointer with the write pointer (wptr).</step>
        <step id="52">Update the buffered write pointer with the current value of the write pointer (wptr) on the rising edge of the write clock (posedge wclk).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Buffer Register for Read Pointer (rptr) -->
    <task>
        <step id="53">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "buffer_read_pointer".</step>
        <step id="54">Declare a register variable rptr_buff representing the buffered read pointer in the range [DEPTH:0].</step>
        <step id="55">Initialize the buffered read pointer with the read pointer (rptr).</step>
        <step id="56">Update the buffered read pointer with the current value of the read pointer (rptr) on the rising edge of the read clock (posedge rclk).</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Comparison Logic for Full Signal Generation -->
    <task>
        <step id="57">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "comparison_for_full_generation".</step>
        <step id="58">Compare the current write pointer (wptr) with the buffered read pointer (rptr_buff).</step>
        <step id="59">Generate the full signal (wfull_gen) based on the comparison results.</step>
    </task>
    <!-- Comparison Logic for Empty Signal Generation -->
    <task>
        <step id="60">Instantiate a sequential logic block named "comparison_for_empty_generation".</step>
        <step id="61">Compare the current read pointer (rptr) with the buffered write pointer (wptr_buff).</step>
        <step id="62">Generate the empty signal (rempty_gen) based on the comparison results.</step>
    </task>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
    // Sequential logic for dual-port RAM
    dual_port_RAM dual_port_ram_inst(
        .wclk(wclk),
        .wenc(winc & !wfull),
        .waddr(waddr),
        .wdata(wdata),
        .rclk(rclk),
        .renc(rinc & !rempty),
        .raddr(raddr),
        .rdata(rdata)
    );
    // Sequential logic for write pointer buffer
    always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
        if (!wrstn) begin
            wptr_buff <= 'd0;
        end else begin
            wptr_buff <= wptr;
        end
    end
    // Sequential logic for read pointer buffer
    always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
        if (!rrstn) begin
            rptr_buff <= 'd0;
        end else begin
            rptr_buff <= rptr;
        end
    end
    // Sequential logic for comparison between write pointer and read pointer buffer for full condition
    always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
        if (!wrstn) begin
            wfull_gen <= 1'd0;
        end else begin
            if ((wptr[3:0] == ~{rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[2:0]})) begin
                wfull_gen <= 1'd1;
            end else begin
                wfull_gen <= 1'd0;
            end
        end
    end
    // Sequential logic for comparison between read pointer and write pointer buffer for empty condition
    always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
        if (!rrstn) begin
            rempty_gen <= 1'd0;
        end else begin
            if ((rptr[3:0] == {wptr_buff[3], wptr_buff[2:0]})) begin
                rempty_gen <= 1'd1;
            end else begin
                rempty_gen <= 1'd0;
            end
        end
    end
    
    endlogic_description>
    <implementation>The dual-port RAM is instantiated separately, and then connected to other blocks such as the data write controller, data read controller, and the synchronizers. The read pointer synchronizer collects the read pointer on the rising edge of the write clock and outputs it to the data write controller. Similarly, the write pointer synchronizer collects the write pointer on the rising edge of the read clock and outputs it to the data read controller. The full and empty signals are generated based on the comparison of the write pointer and read pointer. The write and read addresses are converted to Gray code format for synchronization purposes.</implementation>
</module>
